coredata.dc:title: First-principles Study and Experimental Preparation of LiMn2O4 Cathode Material Based on Spinel Structure
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Liang, Xinghua
Huang, Dongxue
Yuan, Xun
Ji, Fangli
Lan, Lingxiao
Jiang, Xingtao
Wang, Zhenjiang
Fang, Zhijie
Minghua, Li
coredata.dc:description: Spinel-structured LiMn2O4 has three-dimensional lithium ion transport channels, and has become one of the preferred materials for future high-energy and high-power lithium-ion batteries due to its high operating voltage, good stability, and low price. However, its capacity decays rapidly during charging, hindering large-scale commercialization. In order to improve and enhance the cycling performance and capacity of LiMn2O4, it is crucial to correctly understand the atomic properties of the microstructure. In this work, we use density functional theory to investigate the nature of lithium ions and the migration barriers of lithium vacancies in spinel structures. The related compounds were prepared by the sol-gel method, and the battery was assembled to study the performance. The results show that the substitution of Mn atoms in LiMn2O4 by various trivalent, tetravalent and pentavalent cations X (LXM) leads to structural changes affecting Li mobility. The calculated activation energy of the migration vacancy is less than 0.026eV, and for the migration gap in LiMn2O4, the calculated migration energy is about 0.746eV. However, substitution by Ni and Al elements introduces interstitial Li ions for charge compensation, stabilizing the spinel structure and improving the ion transport of LiMn2O4. After experimental studies, it was also verified that the element doping improves the cycle.
coredata.dcterms:subject: LiMn2O4
density functional theory
spinel structures
migration gap
ion transport

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst pubtype ref

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Short Communication First-principles Study and Experimental Preparation of LiMn2 O O4 Cathode Material Based on Spinel StructureXinghua Liang, 1 309602373@qq.com Dongxue Huang, 1 Xun Yuan, 2 Fangli Ji, 3 Lingxiao Lan, 1 Xingtao Jiang, 1 Zhenjiang Wang, 1 Zhijie Fang, 1 Li Minghua, 4 1 Guangxi Key Laboratory of Automobile Components and Vehicle Technology, Guang University of Science & technology, Liuzhou 545006, China; Guangxi Key Laboratory of Automobile Components and Vehicle Technology Guang University of Science & technology Liuzhou 545006 China Guangxi Key Laboratory of Automobile Components and Vehicle Technology, Guang University of Science & technology, Liuzhou 545006, China; 2 Guangxi Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Qinzhou 535000, China; Guangxi Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd Qinzhou 535000 China Guangxi Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Qinzhou 535000, China; 3 Hunan Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Changsha 410000, China; Hunan Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd Changsha 410000 China Hunan Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Changsha 410000, China; 4 School of Electrical Technology, Guangdong Mechanical & Electrical Polytechnic, Guangzhou 510515, China; School of Electrical Technology Guangdong Mechanical & Electrical Polytechnic Guangzhou 510515 China School of Electrical Technology, Guangdong Mechanical & Electrical Polytechnic, Guangzhou 510515, China; Spinel-structured LiMn2 O O4 has three-dimensional lithium ion transport channels, and has become one of the preferred materials for future high-energy and high-power lithium-ion batteries due to its high operating voltage, good stability, and low price. However, its capacity decays rapidly during charging, hindering large-scale commercialization. In order to improve and enhance the cycling performance and capacity of LiMn has three-dimensional lithium ion transport channels, and has become one of the preferred materials for future high-energy and high-power lithium-ion batteries due to its high operating voltage, good stability, and low price. However, its capacity decays rapidly during charging, hindering large-scale commercialization. In order to improve and enhance the cycling performance and capacity of LiMn2 O O4 , it is crucial to correctly understand the atomic properties of the microstructure. In this work, we use density functional theory to investigate the nature of lithium ions and the migration barriers of lithium vacancies in spinel structures. The related compounds were prepared by the sol-gel method, and the battery was assembled to study the performance. The results show that the substitution of Mn atoms in LiMn , it is crucial to correctly understand the atomic properties of the microstructure. In this work, we use density functional theory to investigate the nature of lithium ions and the migration barriers of lithium vacancies in spinel structures. The related compounds were prepared by the sol-gel method, and the battery was assembled to study the performance. The results show that the substitution of Mn atoms in LiMn2 O O4 by various trivalent, tetravalent and pentavalent cations X (LXM) leads to structural changes affecting Li mobility. The calculated activation energy of the migration vacancy is less than 0.026eV, and for the migration gap in LiMn by various trivalent, tetravalent and pentavalent cations X (LXM) leads to structural changes affecting Li mobility. The calculated activation energy of the migration vacancy is less than 0.026eV, and for the migration gap in LiMn2 O O4 , the calculated migration energy is about 0.746eV. However, substitution by Ni and Al elements introduces interstitial Li ions for charge compensation, stabilizing the spinel structure and improving the ion transport of LiMn , the calculated migration energy is about 0.746eV. However, substitution by Ni and Al elements introduces interstitial Li ions for charge compensation, stabilizing the spinel structure and improving the ion transport of LiMn2 O O4 . After experimental studies, it was also verified that the element doping improves the cycle.Keywords LiMn2 O O4 density functional theory spinel structures migration gap ion transport References 1. simple-head.ce:title: First-principles Study and Experimental Preparation of LiMn2O4 Cathode Material Based on Spinel Structure

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Automobile Components and Vehicle Technology, Guang University of Science & technology, Liuzhou 545006, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Automobile Components and Vehicle Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Automobile Components and Vehicle Technology, Guang University of Science & technology, Liuzhou 545006, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Guangxi Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Qinzhou 535000, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Guangxi Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Guangxi Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Qinzhou 535000, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Hunan Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Changsha 410000, China;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Hunan Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Hunan Zhongwei New Energy Technology Co., Ltd, Changsha 410000, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Electrical Technology, Guangdong Mechanical & Electrical Polytechnic, Guangzhou 510515, China;
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Electrical Technology, Guangdong Mechanical & Electrical Polytechnic, Guangzhou 510515, China;

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Spinel-structured LiMn2O4 has three-dimensional lithium ion transport channels, and has become one of the preferred materials for future high-energy and high-power lithium-ion batteries due to its high operating voltage, good stability, and low price. However, its capacity decays rapidly during charging, hindering large-scale commercialization. In order to improve and enhance the cycling performance and capacity of LiMn2O4, it is crucial to correctly understand the atomic properties of the microstructure. In this work, we use density functional theory to investigate the nature of lithium ions and the migration barriers of lithium vacancies in spinel structures. The related compounds were prepared by the sol-gel method, and the battery was assembled to study the performance. The results show that the substitution of Mn atoms in LiMn2O4 by various trivalent, tetravalent and pentavalent cations X (LXM) leads to structural changes affecting Li mobility. The calculated activation energy of the migration vacancy is less than 0.026eV, and for the migration gap in LiMn2O4, the calculated migration energy is about 0.746eV. However, substitution by Ni and Al elements introduces interstitial Li ions for charge compensation, stabilizing the spinel structure and improving the ion transport of LiMn2O4. After experimental studies, it was also verified that the element doping improves the cycle.
